bestPractices: LLMChat is a presentational shell: it renders the conversation, a model picker, and the input, and emits typed messages via onSend. It does not call any AI provider — wire the request (and own the API key) yourself.

The built-in model catalog (DEFAULT_MODELS) is a convenience default that drifts from real provider availability between releases. Pass the `models` prop to advertise the exact set your integration supports.

The optional KeyModal flow and aiKeyStore are a dev-tool convenience: keys are sent browser-direct to the provider and are readable by any script on the page. Use them only for local prototyping or apps where the end user supplies their own session key — never collect production or multi-tenant keys in the browser. See the aiKeyStore threat model.

The message list is a polite live region (role='log', aria-relevant='additions') so screen readers announce new turns as they arrive. While a turn is composing (a message with `typing: true`), the list reports aria-busy so announcements are held until the reply settles — set `typing` on the in-flight assistant message and clear it when the content is final.
The typing indicator honors `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: the bouncing dots stop animating and stay visible at full opacity, so the composing state still reads without motion.
